notable_pick_trades: No 3rd-round pick (traded up for Jaxson Dart in 2025). Acquired 6th-round pick (No. 190) from MIA and 6th-round pick (No. 191) from DAL.
roster_context: New era under HC John Harbaugh after 4-13 2025 season. Building around promising rookie QB Jaxson Dart and elite defensive front (Dexter Lawrence, Brian Burns, Abdul Carter). WR1 Malik Nabers recovering from torn ACL (Week 1 status uncertain). Bobby Okereke released March 3, creating massive LB void. Cap-strapped (~$11M after Okereke cut) with significant free agents departing. Defense was historically bad: 31st in rush defense, 26th in points allowed. Kayvon Thibodeaux trade likely. Numerous roster holes despite strong defensive front and young QB.

needs.explanation: Cor'Dale Flott had a breakout 2025 season but is a UFA projected at $9.5-13M/year. If he leaves, the CB room is questionable. Deonte Banks (former 1st-rounder) has been outplayed by Flott and has not established himself as a reliable starter. Paulson Adebo was signed as a FA last year but provides only one spot. Dru Phillips is solid in the slot but inconsistent. The secondary was a significant part of the defense allowing 25.8 PPG.
needs.current_starter: Paulson Adebo (CB1), Cor'Dale Flott (CB2, UFA), Dru Phillips (slot)
needs.depth: Deonte Banks (disappointing former 1st-rounder), Art Green, Korie Black (7th-round rookie). Thin if Flott departs.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 (or Round 1 if top CB falls)

needs.explanation: Brian Burns and Abdul Carter form one of the NFL's best edge duos. Kayvon Thibodeaux ($14.75M 5th-year option) is actively being shopped — the Giants 'prefer' to trade him per reports, and league sources expect a deal. If Thibodeaux is traded, the Giants gain cap relief and potentially a Day 3 pick, but lose edge depth. Even with a trade, EDGE is not a top-3 need given Burns and Carter.
needs.current_starter: Brian Burns, Abdul Carter (2025 No. 3 overall pick)
needs.depth: Kayvon Thibodeaux (likely traded), Tomon Fox (re-signed). If Thibodeaux goes, depth becomes a concern.
needs.draft_range_target: Day 3 or UDFA for depth only

needs.explanation: RG Greg Van Roten is a 36-year-old UFA. Jon Runyan Jr. and C John Michael Schmitz Jr. have underperformed relative to draft investment. Schmitz ended 2025 on IR (finger). Schlottmann (C) is also a UFA. The Giants are targeting Tyler Linderbaum in free agency but face stiff competition. Even if Schmitz returns, the guard position needs upgrading. The interior OL has been a persistent weakness.
needs.current_starter: Jon Runyan Jr. (LG), John Michael Schmitz Jr. (C), Greg Van Roten (RG, UFA)
needs.depth: Austin Schlottmann (UFA), Jake Kubas (reserve/future). Thin after starters.

needs.explanation: Malik Nabers (torn ACL, Week 4 2025) may not be ready for Week 1 2026 and there is no guarantee of full recovery. Wan'Dale Robinson is a UFA coming off a breakout 1,000-yard season and will command $17-18M/year — likely too expensive for a cap-strapped team. After Nabers and Robinson, the WR room is barren: Darius Slayton is a WR3 at best, Jalin Hyatt has not developed, and Isaiah Hodgins is a UFA. Jaxson Dart desperately needs weapons.
needs.current_starter: Malik Nabers (recovering from ACL), Wan'Dale Robinson (UFA), Darius Slayton
needs.depth: Jalin Hyatt (disappointing), Beaux Collins (unproven). If Robinson leaves, WR2 is a massive void.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (Carnell Tate, Jordyn Tyson, Makai Lemon all in range at No. 5 or No. 37)

needs.explanation: Dexter Lawrence is an elite, untouchable cornerstone at DT. However, behind him there is very little. D.J. Davidson (UFA) and Rakeem Nunez-Roches (UFA) are both departing. The run defense was historically bad (31st). The interior rotation beyond Lawrence needs bodies to help stop the run. Roy Robertson-Harris is a potential cut. Darius Alexander showed some promise late.
needs.depth: Darius Alexander, Marlon Tuipulotu (reserve/future). D.J. Davidson and Rakeem Nunez-Roches both UFAs. Extremely thin behind Lawrence.

needs.explanation: Dane Belton is a UFA after starting alongside Tyler Nubin. Nubin ended 2025 on IR with a neck injury — his status for 2026 needs monitoring. If Belton departs and Nubin's health is uncertain, safety becomes a significant need. The Giants are linked to Caleb Downs (Ohio State) at No. 5 in multiple mocks. Even if Nubin is healthy, the second safety spot needs addressing.
needs.current_starter: Tyler Nubin (under contract, but ended 2025 on IR with neck), Dane Belton (UFA)
needs.depth: K'Von Wallace (signed), Patrick McMorris (reserve/future). Very thin.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (Caleb Downs at No. 5 is a possibility)

needs.explanation: Bobby Okereke was released on March 3, 2026, saving $9M in cap space. Micah McFadden is a UFA coming off a season-ending foot injury. Only Chris Board and Darius Muasau are under contract — neither profiles as a starter. Giants were 31st in rush defense (145.3 YPG) and 32nd in yards per rush (5.3) in 2025. John Harbaugh historically prioritizes elite LB play (Ray Lewis, C.J. Mosby, Roquan Smith lineage in Baltimore). This is the single biggest hole on the roster and Harbaugh's top schematic priority.
needs.depth: Chris Board, Darius Muasau — neither is a viable starter. Dyontae Johnson re-signed but is a depth piece only.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (Sonny Styles at No. 5 is in play) or address in FA + Round 2

needs.explanation: RT Jermaine Eluemunor is a UFA expected to command $8.7-21M/year on the open market. If he departs, the Giants have no proven RT. Andrew Thomas (LT) has significant injury concerns — missed time in 2024 (Lisfranc) and ended 2025 on IR (hamstring) after playing only 13-14 games. Rookie Marcus Mbow (5th-round pick) showed flashes but is still raw and needs development. The offensive line was a top-10 unit when healthy but is extremely fragile. Protecting Jaxson Dart is paramount.
needs.current_starter: Andrew Thomas (LT, under contract but major injury history), Jermaine Eluemunor (RT, UFA)
needs.depth: Marcus Mbow (2025 5th-rounder, can play LT/RT), James Hudson (likely cut candidate at $5.4M). Extremely thin if Eluemunor leaves.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (Spencer Fano, Francis Mauigoa could be available at No. 5) or Round 2

free_agency_impact: Signed Isaiah Likely (TE) from Baltimore Ravens — $13.3M AAV
Signed Tremaine Edmunds (LB) from Chicago Bears — $12.0M AAV
Signed Jermaine Eluemunor (OT) from New York Giants (re-sign) — $13.0M AAV
Signed Jordan Stout (P) from Baltimore Ravens — $4.1M AAV
Signed Ar'Darius Washington (S) from Baltimore Ravens — $3.0M AAV
Signed Chris Manhertz (TE) from New York Giants (re-sign) — $2.0M AAV
Signed Gunner Olszewski (WR) from New York Giants (re-sign) — $2.0M AAV
Signed Isaiah Hodgins (WR) from New York Giants (re-sign) — $2.0M AAV
scheme_fit_notes: John Harbaugh is installing a new defensive system under DC Dennard Wilson (formerly Titans DC). Harbaugh historically ran aggressive, physical defenses in Baltimore built around elite LB play (Ray Lewis, C.J. Mosby, Roquan Smith). Expect a defense that values versatile, athletic LBs who can cover and rush — Sonny Styles fits this mold perfectly. Offensive coordinator Matt Nagy (former Chiefs OC/Bears HC) will likely run a system that relies on play-action off a strong run game, making OL quality and WR ability after the catch premium traits. The coaching staff's Baltimore DNA means physical, run-first football — IOL maulers and road-grading OTs will be prioritized over finesse players.
